title: Boat Rental in Corfu: A Day on the Ionian Sea
html:
Exploring Corfu by boat is the finest way to experience the Ionian Sea, with departures from Gouvia Marina offering modern facilities and easy access to secluded bays. Prices for a half-day rental start from around €250 for a small motorboat, and you can compare verified options through BoatHire24.

Where to set off in Corfu

Corfu has several excellent departure points. Gouvia Marina is the largest, with full facilities and a range of charter companies. Nearby, the old port of Corfu Town offers a more traditional start, while Kassiopi harbour in the north-east gives quick access to Albanian coastline views. Further south, Lefkimmi port serves the quieter southern bays. For island hopping, you can cross to Paxos, about 30 nautical miles south, or explore the Diapontia Islands to the north-west.

Which boat to rent

  1. Axopar 22, ideal for a couple or small family, exhilarating speedboat for exploring caves; up to 6 guests; from €350 half-day.
  2. Lagoon 40 catamaran, stable and spacious, perfect for a day sail with friends; up to 10 guests; from €600 full-day.
  3. Maiora 26m, a luxury motor yacht with a flybridge and crew; for a special occasion; up to 12 guests; from €3,500 full-day (see the Maiora 26m charter page for details).
  4. Fairline Targa 38, a sporty cruiser for experienced skippers; up to 8 guests; from €800 half-day.
  5. Sunseeker Predator 68, a powerful open yacht with luxury finishes; for a VIP experience; up to 10 guests; from €2,500 half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Corfu?

Prices vary by boat type and season, but expect from €200 for a shared tour to over €5,000 for a crewed superyacht.

  • Shared sunset tour: €50, €90 per person, typically 3 hours, max 12 guests.
  • Small motorboat (e.g. 4.5m): €250 half-day, up to 5 guests.
  • Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 40): €700 full-day, up to 10 guests.
  • Full-day crewed yacht with skipper: from €1,500 for a 12m yacht, up to 8 guests.

You can browse skippered charters in Corfu on the marketplace for up-to-date availability and prices.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Corfu?

UK licence holders must hold an ICC or equivalent; operators will confirm exact requirements before booking.

Motorboats over 30hp typically require a licence, but many smaller boats can be rented without one. Skippered charters remove all licensing concerns.

In high season (July and August), the north-easterly Maestro wind picks up in the afternoon, so plan your anchoring spots accordingly, early morning is best for calm conditions.

At a glance

  • Price band: €200, €5,000 per day
  • Peak months: July and August
  • Trip length: 4 hours to full day
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests (most boats)
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Corfu

Can I rent a boat for a week in Corfu?

Yes, many bareboat and crewed charters are available for weekly hire, especially from Gouvia Marina, with prices starting around €1,500 per week for a small sailboat.

What is the best time to rent a boat in Corfu?

May, June and September offer warm weather and fewer crowds, while the sea temperature is pleasant for swimming from June onwards.

How far is it from Corfu to Paxos by boat?

The distance from Corfu Town to Gaios in Paxos is approximately 15 nautical miles, a scenic one-hour cruise on a motorboat.

Whether you are a seasoned sailor or a first-timer, renting a boat in Corfu opens up a world of turquoise coves and historic islands. Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Corfu and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
